name: The Millyard Bank
description_short: Independent community bank in Nashua, NH offering personal banking, business banking, and commercial lending with local decision-making and FDIC insurance.
description_long:
Millyard Bank is a locally-owned and independently-operated community bank established in December 2019 by individuals, professionals, and business owners in the Nashua, New Hampshire area. The bank was created to serve local residents and businesses underserved by larger national banks that have consolidated and centralized their lending decisions. The founders identified a gap in the market where small to medium-sized businesses struggle to secure funding due to minimum lending thresholds and remote decision-making processes at larger institutions.

Millyard Bank offers three primary service lines: Personal Banking for individual customers managing savings and checking needs; Business Banking for small to medium-sized business deposit and operational services; and Commercial Lending for business loan needs. All deposits are FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank operates two physical banking centers—one in Nashua at 57 Northeastern Boulevard and one in Hollis at 9 Ash Street—and provides online banking with enrollment and login capabilities for both retail and business customers.

What distinguishes Millyard Bank from larger competitors is its commitment to local ownership, management, and decision-making. The company emphasizes that employees, directors, and shareholders all work, live, and play locally and understand the regional market. Lending decisions are made by people who know their customers personally, rather than by centralized loan centers located outside the community. This approach targets the specific pain point of small business owners who receive inadequate attention at national banks and face arbitrary lending minimums that exclude them from financing opportunities.

Millyard Bank is genuinely positioned as a niche community bank for local Nashua-area customers and small businesses. However, the website provides limited detail on specific loan products, interest rates, fees, account types, and lending terms. Prospective customers must contact the bank directly or visit a branch to understand specific offerings, minimum deposits, loan qualification criteria, and competitive pricing. The bank's two-location footprint also limits accessibility compared to national or regional competitors with broader branch networks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal banking checking and savings accounts
Business banking deposit and transaction services
Commercial lending for small and medium-sized businesses
Online banking login portal for retail customers
Online banking login portal for business customers
In-person banking at Nashua Banking Center
In-person banking at Hollis Banking Center
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Locally-owned and independently-operated with no out-of-state corporate parent
Lending decisions made by local relationship managers, not remote centralized loan centers
Established by local business professionals specifically to serve local business professionals
Offers commercial lending to small and medium-sized businesses—a demographic underserved by large banks
Two convenient branch locations in Nashua and Hollis with local phone support
Online banking enrollment and login available for both personal and business customers
cons: Only two physical branch locations limits accessibility compared to regional and national competitors
Website provides no information on specific checking/savings account features, fees, or minimum balances
No interest rates published on website—customers must contact bank directly for pricing
Established only in December 2019, so limited track record and brand recognition compared to established competitors
Limited online presence regarding specific loan products, terms, approval criteria, and commercial lending options
best_for: Small to medium-sized business owners seeking relationship-based lending and local decision-making
Nashua and southern New Hampshire residents wanting to bank with a locally-owned institution
Business owners frustrated by minimum lending thresholds and impersonal service at national banks
Personal banking customers prioritizing local community investment and personalized service
diagnosis: Millyard Bank is best for small-to-medium business owners and Nashua-area residents who prioritize local ownership and relationship-based banking over the convenience of large branch networks. The main caveat is limited online transparency regarding specific products, rates, and terms—customers must contact the bank directly or visit a branch to compare offerings and make informed decisions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
